summary: Young professionals in India are opting for short weekend treks instead of long vacations. &nbsp;Weekend trips offer affordability and accessibility, while organizations emphasize the importance of physical and financial preparation for trekking.

summary: Arushi Garg, a 23-year-old JPMorgan Chase engineer, shared her monthly expenses on Instagram, revealing the high cost of living in Bengaluru. Her total expenses range from ₹30,000 to ₹35,000, sparking discussions about financial pressures faced by young professionals in urban areas.

title: Kaun Banega Crorepati: But ₹1 crore in 2046 won’t be enough — plan for inflation‑proof retirement. Here's how

title: Hajj can cost up to ₹10 lakh, Umrah starts at ₹1.5 lakh: What drives the huge price difference in Mecca pilgrimages?
summary: Cost is a key factor for pilgrims deciding between Hajj and Umrah. Hajj is a mandatory religious duty and significantly more expensive than Umrah, which is a recommended practice and more flexible in scheduling and pricing.

title: ITR filing 2026: Can super senior citizens avoid filing income tax? Exemptions, conditions and rules explained
summary: Super senior citizens may not need to file income tax returns if they meet conditions under Section 194P. Complete exemption applies to those 75+ with only a pension and interest income from a specified bank. Details here.&nbsp;

title: RBI updates e-mandate norms: No OTP needed for recurring payments up to ₹15,000 — here's what it means for users
summary: RBI on Tuesday issued a consolidated digital payments e-mandate framework, setting a threshold of ₹15,000 per transaction for recurring payments without requiring an OTP. The framework includes provisions for cross-border payments and mandates pre-debit alerts, among other measures. Details here.
